sizing_rule.note: Position size must be reduced whenever HPR approaches or equals zero to preserve positive equity. This is a protective constraint ensuring HPR always remains > 0, effectively triggering position reduction when system drawdown would cause HPR collapse.

source_rule_text: Avoid position sizing that results in an HPR (Holding Period Return) of zero, as this completely wipes out the account; position sizing must maintain positive equity preservation relative to system dispersion.
